title: Tractable Problems in AI Security via Formal Methods

execution-harness.label: Execution Harness
execution-harness.desc: The outermost software layer: inference servers, sandboxes, and API gateways that handle model I/O.

software-framework.label: Software & ML Framework
software-framework.desc: Frameworks (PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow), ML compilers (TVM, XLA, MLIR), and the dependency supply chain.

orchestration-cloud.label: Orchestration & Cloud
orchestration-cloud.desc: Cluster orchestration (K8s, Slurm, Ray), network fabric (InfiniBand, SDN), distributed protocols, and IAM.

firmware-lowlevel.label: Firmware & Low-Level Systems
firmware-lowlevel.desc: Hypervisors, GPU device drivers, CUDA/ROCm runtimes, and boot integrity / remote attestation.

hardware-supply-chain.label: Hardware & Physical Supply Chain
hardware-supply-chain.desc: Chip RTL, EDA toolchains, hardware root of trust, secure enclaves, and physical supply chain integrity.

categories.enabler: Problems whose resolution unbottlenecks a class of downstream work. Solving an enabler doesn't itself ship an artifact, but failing to solve it degrades everything that depends on it.
categories.widget: Concrete, scoped deliverables: a verified runtime, a proven-correct sampler, a formally checked allocator. Each stands on its own as a security improvement to a specific stack layer.
